text:
Once upon a time, there was a curious badger named Bramble. Bramble loved to explore the forest, especially the odd-looking bird called a keystone. One morning, Bramble discovered an old treasure chest embedded deep in an old oak tree. All sorts of shiny things sparkled inside. Bramble picked up a pretty emerald. That night, a gentle voice beckoned him into a clearing.

On the edge of a pond sat a wise old owl. She gave Bramble a map.

"You find treasure," she said. "It says here the gemstone helps you find what you're curious about."

Bramble found the tiny emerald lost under a berry bush and kept it. Years passed, and Bramble became a respected wise old owl himself.

Humans heard tales of Bramble's wisdom, came just to hear him talk about nature. One day, a king wanted to build the best memorial owl owl statue ever. Bramble said the headcrest should think about owl love
